description: Curated queries mapped to ground-truth obligations, organised by tier. Use these as the benchmark question set for the Chroma vs Neo4j comparison.

A.label: Flat retrieval — should be a draw
A.rationale: Questions answerable from a single chunk. High Jaccard expected. Both systems should score equally. Use these as the control group.

queries.question: What is the risk management system obligation on providers of high-risk AI systems under Article 9 of the EU AI Act?
What human oversight requirements must providers build into high-risk AI systems under Article 14?
What obligations does a deployer of a high-risk AI system have under Article 26?
What data processing principles does the Swiss FADP impose on controllers?
What data security breach notification obligations exist under the Swiss FADP?
When must users be informed that they are interacting with an AI system under EU AI Act Article 50?
What human responsibility obligations do the Swiss AI Guidelines impose on the Federal Administration?

B.label: Community/thematic — Chroma advantage (if community search is wired up)
B.rationale: Thematic questions spanning multiple chunks and one or two documents. The correct answer lives in a regulatory theme cluster. Chroma community embeddings should surface the theme first; Neo4j should retrieve via graph expansion but may miss cross-chunk thematic context.

queries.question: What is the complete set of obligations a provider of a high-risk AI system must fulfill before placing it on the EU market?
queries.note: Tests compound Article 16 chain. Chroma community covering 'Provider Obligations' should surface all sub-items.

queries.question: What elements must a provider's quality management system cover under Article 17 of the EU AI Act?
What obligations apply to providers of general-purpose AI models under Article 53?
Under the Swiss FADP, when must a controller carry out a data protection impact assessment and what must it cover?
What fundamental rights impact assessment must a public body deployer perform before deploying a high-risk AI system?
How do the Swiss AI Guidelines require federal bodies to ensure FADP compliance when using AI systems that process personal data?
queries.note: Cross-document bridge: Swiss AI Guidelines to FADP. Medium difficulty.

C.label: Multi-hop relational — Neo4j advantage
C.rationale: Questions that require chaining across multiple entities or obligation nodes. The answer is only fully correct if the system traverses Provider → OBLIGATION → ConformityAssessment → REQUIRES → CE marking, or similar chains. Neo4j's native Cypher graph traversal should outperform the flat KG sidecar.

queries.question: What is the complete compliance chain a provider must follow from developing a high-risk AI system to placing it on the market — tracing all intermediate steps and their article references?
queries.note: THE key multi-hop test: Art.16 → Art.17 → Art.43 → Art.47 → Art.48 → Art.49

queries.question: Under what circumstances does a distributor or importer become subject to provider obligations for a high-risk AI system, and what obligations does this trigger?
queries.note: Role-shift multi-hop: Distributor → substantial modification → becomes Provider → Art.16 obligations

queries.question: What additional obligations apply to providers of general-purpose AI models identified as posing systemic risk, and who do they report serious incidents to?
queries.note: Multi-hop: GPAI → systemic risk designation → additional obligations (Art.55) → reporting to AI Office

D.label: Cross-document bridge — hard for both
D.rationale: Questions requiring facts from two different source documents that were chunked separately. Neither system will naturally bridge these unless cross-document entity merging was done. Neo4j has a theoretical advantage if entities are globally merged; in practice both should struggle, making this the most informative tier for the showcase finding.

queries.question: A Swiss company that develops AI-embedded products and exports them to EU markets — what EU AI Act obligations do they face, and does the Switzerland-EU MRA affect conformity assessment?
queries.note: Cross-doc: Swiss_AI_Guidelines (MRA context) + EU_AI_Act (conformity obligations). Hardest query.

queries.question: A Swiss bank uses an AI system for automated credit scoring decisions. What obligations arise under both the Swiss FADP and the EU AI Act?
queries.note: Cross-doc bridge: FADP Art. 21 (automated decision) + EU AI Act high-risk deployer obligations

queries.question: Compare how the Swiss FADP and the EU AI Act each address transparency obligations when AI systems make or support individual decisions.
queries.note: Hardest cross-doc comparison: FADP transparency + EU AIA Art.13 + Art.50
